description: This figure presents a combined view of the experimental setup for nano-indentation mapping near grain boundaries in SPCE cold-rolled steel sheets. The left panel shows an optical microscopy image of the measurement region before Chemical Mechanical Polishing (CMP) treatment, displaying a large ferritic grain with visible grain boundaries (dark etched lines) and a coordinate system with origin O positioned in the grain interior. The right panel illustrates the measurement point layout for nano-indentation testing, showing a grid of measurement points at coordinates X=0, 1, 2 and Y=0, 1, 2 with units in micrometers (µm), indicating systematic indentation positions relative to the grain boundary. The sample was prepared by etching to reveal grain boundaries and Vickers indentation marking for position reference prior to CMP surface flattening. This imaging supports the investigation of spatial variation in elastic modulus across grain interiors and boundary regions, providing the microstructural context for subsequent nano-indentation measurements that revealed decreased elastic modulus near grain boundaries due to dislocation pile-up effects.
